summary: Research on whether UNGA will pass resolution condemning US Venezuela operation before Feb 1, 2026
content:
## Key Facts
- US military operation captured Maduro on Jan 3, 2026
- UN Security Council emergency meeting held Jan 5, 2026
- Broad condemnation from allies (France) and adversaries
- UN Secretary General said US violated the UN Charter
- UN experts formally condemned the action on Jan 7

## Historical Precedents
- Panama 1989: Invasion Dec 20 → UNSC veto Dec 23 → UNGA Resolution 44/240 passed Dec 29 (9 days)
- Grenada 1983: Invasion Oct 25 → UNGA Resolution 38/7 passed within ~2 weeks
- Ukraine 2022: Invasion Feb 24 → UNGA ES-11/1 passed Mar 2 (6 days)
- Iraq 2003: NO UNGA condemnation despite massive global opposition (but had coalition, complex prior UN involvement)

## Current Status (Jan 11)
- No evidence found of a draft UNGA resolution on Venezuela
- No evidence of an Emergency Special Session being called
- Security Council meeting was discussion only, no resolution vote found
- 20 days remain until Feb 1 deadline

## Key Differences from Precedents
- Venezuela more similar to Panama (unilateral, leader capture on drug charges)
- Iraq 2003 is poor comparison (had coalition, complex UN history)
- But 8 days elapsed without visible UNGA action (Panama took 9 days total)
- Maduro was unpopular (but sovereignty principle still applies)
- Trump administration may use aggressive diplomatic pressure
